title: mentha longifolia subsp. royleana
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. royleana, also known as Indian pennyroyal or long-leaved pennyroyal, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia subsp. royleana include its ability to relieve digestive issues, ease menstrual cramps, and reduce stress and anxiety, while also acting as an insect repellent. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be used for various medicinal purposes, although caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential interactions with certain medications.
